description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a dark, almost black coloration interspersed with brownish and lighter gray patches. Its surface is rough and uneven, featuring sharp edges and angular facets, suggesting it may have been chipped or fractured rather than naturally rounded. One side of the stone displays a patch of white paint or marking, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes. The stone's texture appears hard and dense, with some areas showing subtle mottling and minor surface pitting. There are no visible signs of artificial shaping beyond the rough breakage, and the overall form is asymmetrical. The stone would likely be found as a loose obstacle or barrier in an urban park, roadside, or construction area.
description: A dark, angular stone with rough, chipped surfaces and lighter patches, including a visible white marking on one side. Its irregular shape and hard texture make it a natural-looking urban obstacle.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's pointed end and the dark, jagged surface with some brownish hues.
description_view_1: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible here, showing mottled dark and brown coloration with angular edges.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the stone's blunt end and the overall blocky, uneven geometry, with less visible surface detail.
description_view_3: A white marking or label is prominent on this side, contrasting with the otherwise dark, rough surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and veining with irregular, natural fracture lines; one side has a painted or marked patch.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped with minor surface pitting, indicating moderate exposure but not extensive erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban surfaces, may partially blend with soil or gravel but stands out due to its dark color and angular form.
